name: AORTIC PROSTHETIC GRAFT INFECTION
dominant_finding: uptake of 99mTc-hexametazine labeled leukocytes (drawbacks: not performed quickly,
distribution: focal bowel wall thickening → indicates fistula
discriminating_features: ring of fat attenuation in early postoperative period < 5 mm between aneurysm wall and
perigraft soft-tissue attenuation with indistinctness of graft margins
ectopic gas ← fistulous communication with bowel / gas-producing organism

general: ring of fat attenuation in early postoperative period < 5 mm between aneurysm wall and
perigraft soft-tissue attenuation with indistinctness of graft margins
ectopic gas ← fistulous communication with bowel / gas-producing organism
focal bowel wall thickening → indicates fistula
> 5 mm soft tissue between graft + surrounding wrap (beyond 7th postoperative week)
focal discontinuity of calcified aneurysmal wrap
NUC: uptake of 99mTc-hexametazine labeled leukocytes (drawbacks: not performed quickly,
